title: Fluffy Portuguese Smoky Paella
description: A smoky and fluffy twist on traditional paella, inspired by Portuguese flavors and egg-free
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or paella pan over medium-high heat.
Sear the ground turkey until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the onion and garlic to the pan, cooking until the onion is translucent.
Add the smoked paprika, oregano, and demerara sugar, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the chickpeas, tomato paste, and chicken broth to the pan, stirring to combine.
Add the brown rice to the pan, stirring to coat with the flavorful liquid.
Transfer the pan to the oven, or cover with foil and simmer on the stovetop over low heat for 25 minutes.
Remove the pan from the oven, or remove the foil from the stovetop pan.
Fluff the paella with a fork, then cover with foil to rest for 5 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with parsley or cilantro if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
